pattern: Finding the first position where one string occurs inside another is the classic substring-search problem, solvable by scanning candidate start offsets.
hints: You are looking for the leftmost place where the needle lines up exactly with a slice of the haystack.
Only start positions where the remaining haystack is at least as long as the needle can possibly match.
For each start index i from 0 to len(haystack)-len(needle), compare haystack[i:i+len(needle)] to the needle and return the first i that matches, else -1.

edge_inputs.description: needle is a single character found late
partial match then full match later
needle longer than haystack cannot match

stress.description: random haystack over a tiny alphabet with a needle that sometimes occurs
stress.note: tiny alphabet forces many near-matches; needle often a real substring
